methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $262M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Exotec
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

private.name: Agility Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Outrider, a Series D robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $262M to date, sits within a competitive cohort of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable. The peer set includes same-stage (Exotec at $2.0B Series D) and adjacent-stage companies (Divergent at $2.3B Series E; Hadrian and Nimble Robotics at $1.6B and $1.0B respectively, both Series C), spanning valuations from $1.0B to $2.3B.

Two valuation methods triangulate a range. The stage-progression method applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, using the $262M total raised as a baseline proxy, yielding $393M. The peer-median approach anchors to the four-company robotics cohort, which trades at a median valuation of $1.8B with a $1.0B–$2.3B range. These methods produce a triangulated low of $334M, median of $1.1B, and high of $2.1B.

The wide spread reflects meaningful heterogeneity: the peer-median implies substantially higher valuations than stage-progression alone, likely reflecting capital intensity and market maturation within the sector. The valuation range of $1.1B (median) is most defensible, though Outrider's specific position within the $334M–$2.1B band depends on traction metrics, revenue scale, and capital efficiency relative to identified peers. Key uncertainties include revenue run-rate, gross margins, and customer concentration relative to Exotec and Divergent.
